content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a Shape Area Calculator using Abstract Base Classes in Python. The goal is to implement a system that can calculate the areas of different shapes.

You need to create an abstract base class called `Shape` with an abstract method `calculate_area()`. Then, implement two concrete classes, `Circle` and `Rectangle`, which inherit from the `Shape` class and implement the `calculate_area()` method.

The program should take input for the type of shape and its dimensions, then calculate and display the area.

Example:
Input:
circle
5

Output:
78.54
constraints: All inputs are valid and correspond to the described shape.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line is the shape type as a lowercase string: "circle" or "rectangle". If the shape is "circle", the second line contains a single non‑negative real number R (the radius). If the shape is "rectangle", the second line contains two non‑negative real numbers L and W separated by a space (the length and width).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with the area of the given shape, rounded to exactly two decimal places.

solution.approach: Define an abstract base class Shape with an abstract method calculate_area(). Implement Circle and Rectangle classes that inherit from Shape and override calculate_area() using the formulas area = π·r² and area = l·w respectively. For each test case read the shape type, instantiate the appropriate class, compute the area, and print it with two decimal places. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored per test case.
